name: First National Bank In Ord
description_short: Community bank serving central Nebraska for 140+ years with locations in Ord, Greeley, and Sargent. Offers full-service banking including checking, savings, lending, and agricultural financial services.
description_long:
First National Bank in Ord was founded on March 16, 1882, as the Ord City Bank and received its national charter in 1885. The bank has grown from its original single location to serve Valley County and adjacent counties across central Nebraska with three branch offices. The institution has survived major economic challenges including the farm crisis of the 1980s, during which it acquired the Farmers State Bank of Sargent in 1985. Today, it operates as a locally-owned, full-service national bank with FDIC insurance backing all deposits.

First National Bank in Ord provides traditional banking services including checking and savings accounts, online banking with bill pay functionality, mobile deposit capabilities, debit cards, and lending services tailored to agricultural customers. The bank offers modern digital conveniences such as remote check deposit via mobile app, online and telephone banking, and drive-up services across all three locations. Their website emphasizes online security features, identity theft protection, and cyber security education for customers.

The bank distinguishes itself through deep community roots and local ownership, with each branch staffed by experienced banking professionals. They emphasize personalized, hometown customer service and pride themselves on being "Solid As A Rock" with sound lending policies. The institution actively educates customers about security threats and provides specific resources including CardValet protection, security alerts, and identity theft protection guidance.

As a small regional bank, First National Bank in Ord serves customers who value community banking relationships and agricultural lending expertise. The main limitation is geographic reach—they operate only three branches in rural Nebraska. Digital capabilities exist but lag larger institutions, with limitations such as discontinued ATM deposits at the drive-up location in Ord, pushing customers toward mobile deposit or night deposit boxes. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full government backing
140+ years of continuous operation and financial stability
Specialized agricultural lending and banking services for farm customers
Three branch locations with extended drive-up hours (8:00 AM - 4:00 PM weekdays, Saturday hours)
Mobile deposit app for remote check deposits
Online banking with bill pay functionality
No-cost online and telephone banking access
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to three rural Nebraska locations
Discontinued ATM deposits at Ord drive-up location, reducing convenience
Limited evening/weekend lobby hours (3:00 PM weekday closing most locations)
Small institution with likely fewer digital features compared to regional or national banks
No mention of investment products, wealth management, or advanced financial services
best_for: Agricultural businesses and farm operators in central Nebraska seeking specialized lending
Rural Nebraska residents who prioritize local ownership and community relationships
Customers in Valley County, Ord, Greeley, or Sargent who value in-person service
Traditional banking customers who prefer established institutions with 140+ year track records
diagnosis: Best for rural Nebraska customers seeking reliable community banking with agricultural expertise and local decision-making. Primary caveat: geographic limitation to three small-town branches and limited digital/modern banking features compared to larger institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
